blockingIssues.description: _ensure_obj_desc_inst() still returns after finding any obj_desc_inst row for the object instead of ensuring the required (object, desc_inst) pair exists, so deep_upsert(values_quant/values_cat, ...) can still violate the composite FK when the object already has a different descriptor mapping.

The new trigger-ordering tests only cover objects with no obj_desc_inst rows at all and do not exercise the remaining broken case where an object already has a different obj_desc_inst row, leaving the blocking gap unverified.
